title: Method for sustainable human cognitive enhancement
abstract: A method of achieving sustainable, general-purpose cognitive enhancement in mentally-healthy adults comprising administering a gene-editing endonuclease complexed with a synthetic guide RNA to lower the population of 5-hydroxytryptamine 2A receptors in the brain.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for general-purpose cognitive enhancement in mentally healthy adults comprising administering a gene-editing endonuclease complexed with a synthetic guide RNA to lower the population of 5-hydroxytryptamine 2A receptors in the brain. 2. A method as recited in claim 1 where a catalytically active gene-editing endonuclease is complexed with a synthetic guide RNA to lower the population of 5-hydroxytryptamine 2A receptors in the brain by deleting gene HTR2A from CNS neurons. 3. A method as recited in claim 1 where a catalytically inactive gene-editing endonuclease is complexed with a synthetic guide RNA to lower the population of 5-hydroxytryptamine 2A receptors in the brain by interfering with the transcription or translation of gene HTR2A into cellular proteins for building 5-hydroxytryptamine 2A receptors.
